Frequently Asked Questions

The main idea is not simply that something strange happened. It is that internet glitches become folklore when users save, retell, and compare them as stories instead of closing the tab gives the story a concrete shape, making the folklore development motif easy to remember and retell.

What evidence would make how internet glitches become folklore instead of error reports more credible?

Useful evidence would include dated early versions, publication history, oral-history notes, archive copies, and clear links between variants. A repeated rumor can prove circulation, but it does not automatically prove the event or claim inside the rumor.
